title: Ansible Playbook Failure Tracking
spl:
index=iac sourcetype="ansible:result"
| stats sum(failed) as fails, sum(unreachable) as unreach, count as runs by playbook
| eval fail_rate=round((fails+unreach)/runs*100,2)
| where fail_rate > 5
| sort -fail_rate
description: Failure rate per playbook and run‚Äîtrending layer on top of UC-12.4.3 outcomes.
value: Failure rate per playbook and run‚Äîtrending layer on top of UC-12.4.3 outcomes.
implementation: Alert when fail_rate >5% over 24h. Page for security baseline playbooks. Host-level drilldown from same data.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Ansible Splunk callback, ARA.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Per-play `failed`, `unreachable`, `playbook`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Alert when fail_rate >5% over 24h. Page for security baseline playbooks. Host-level drilldown from same data.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iac sourcetype="ansible:result"
| stats sum(failed) as fails, sum(unreachable) as unreach, count as runs by playbook
| eval fail_rate=round((fails+unreach)/runs*100,2)
| where fail_rate > 5
| sort -fail_rate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Ansible Playbook Failure Tracking** ‚Äî Failure rate per playbook and run‚Äîtrending layer on top of UC-12.4.3 outcomes.

Documented **Data sources**: Per-play `failed`, `unreachable`, `playbook`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Ansible Splunk callback, ARA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iac; **sourcetype**: ansible:result. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=iac, sourcetype="ansible:result". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by playbook** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_rate** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where fail_rate > 5` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (fail rate trend), Table (worst playbooks), Bar chart (by team).
visualization: Line chart (fail rate trend), Table (worst playbooks), Bar chart (by team).
